items.definition_en: Surprise is a feeling you get when something happens that you did not expect.
items.example_en: Her birthday party was a big surprise for her.

items.definition_en: Curiosity is the feeling of wanting to know or learn about something.
items.example_en: Children often show great curiosity about the world.

items.definition_en: Tension is a feeling of nervousness or worry.
items.example_en: There was tension in the room before the results were announced.

items.definition_en: Wonder is a feeling of amazement and admiration.
items.example_en: The children looked at the fireworks with wonder.

items.definition_en: Shock is a strong feeling of surprise and fear.
items.example_en: He was in shock after hearing the bad news.

items.definition_en: Desire is a strong feeling of wanting something.
items.example_en: She has a strong desire to travel the world.

items.definition_en: Longing is the feeling of wanting something very much.
items.example_en: She felt a deep longing for her hometown.

items.definition_en: Anger is a strong feeling of being upset or annoyed.
items.example_en: She could not hide her anger during the meeting.

items.definition_en: Warmth is a feeling of kindness and friendliness.
items.example_en: He felt warmth from her smile.

items.definition_en: Ease is a feeling of being comfortable or relaxed.
items.example_en: She spoke with ease in front of the class.

items.definition_en: Fearlessness is the feeling of not being afraid at all.
items.example_en: His fearlessness helped him climb the mountain.

items.definition_en: Relief is a feeling of happiness because something difficult is over.
items.example_en: They felt great relief when the exam was finished.

items.definition_en: Adoration is a strong feeling of love and respect.
items.example_en: The fans showed their adoration for the singer.

items.definition_en: Disbelief is the feeling of not being able to accept something as true.
items.example_en: She looked at the results in disbelief.

items.definition_en: Contentment is the feeling of being happy and satisfied.
items.example_en: He smiled with contentment after a good meal.

items.definition_en: Resentment is the feeling of anger or unhappiness because you think you have been treated badly.
items.example_en: She felt resentment after being left out of the group.

items.definition_en: Pleasure is a feeling of happiness or enjoyment.
items.example_en: Eating ice cream gives me pleasure.

items.definition_en: Melancholy is a feeling of gentle sadness.
items.example_en: A sense of melancholy filled the quiet room.
